title: Ping Graph Feature - PRD
description: Add a real-time ping graph using Chart.js that displays last 5 minutes of latency data, updated every second

items.description: Install Chart.js and react-chartjs-2 packages for React chart rendering
acceptance_criteria: chart.js@^4.5.1 is installed and listed in package.json dependencies
react-chartjs-2@^5.3.1 is installed and listed in package.json dependencies
Both packages are importable without errors in the application
steps_to_verify: Check package.json contains both chart.js and react-chartjs-2 in dependencies
Run 'bun install' and verify no errors occur
Attempt to import Chart.js components in a test file without TypeScript errors

items.description: Create usePingLatency custom hook to measure HTTP latency every second with 5-minute rolling buffer
acceptance_criteria: Hook measures actual HTTP request time to /status.json endpoint
Measurements occur every 1000ms (1 second)
Maintains exactly 300 data points representing last 5 minutes
Failed pings are stored as null values (gaps)
Successful pings store latency in milliseconds
Each data point includes timestamp and latency
Server status data refreshes separately every 5 seconds
Hook cleans up intervals and aborts pending requests on unmount
steps_to_verify: Check that setInterval is called with 1000ms delay
Verify data array never exceeds 300 items
Simulate a failed request and verify null is stored in data
Simulate a successful request and verify latency number is stored
Unmount component and verify clearInterval is called
Check that AbortController aborts pending requests

items.description: Create PingGraph component with Chart.js line graph showing latency over time
acceptance_criteria: Line chart displays latency data from usePingLatency hook
X-axis shows time labels (HH:MM:SS format in French locale)
Y-axis shows latency in milliseconds (auto-scaling with 500ms base)
Line color transitions from green (<100ms) to yellow (100-300ms) to red (>300ms)
Chart fills area under line with gradient (green to red)
Failed pings appear as gaps in the line (spanGaps: true)
Chart has no animation for smooth 1-second updates
Chart is responsive and fits container width
Height is fixed at 128px (h-32)
Legend is hidden
Tooltip shows formatted time and latency in French
Tooltip uses medieval theme colors (amber title, stone background)
steps_to_verify: Render component with sample data and verify chart appears
Check X-axis labels match HH:MM:SS format
Verify Y-axis shows 'ms' suffix on ticks
Test with latency 50ms - verify green color
Test with latency 200ms - verify yellow/orange color
Test with latency 400ms - verify red color
Insert null value in data and verify gap appears in line
Verify chart height is exactly 128px
Hover over data point and verify tooltip shows 'Latence: X ms'
Check that animation duration is set to 0

items.description: PingGraph UI section with latency indicator and legend
acceptance_criteria: Section has medieval theme styling matching existing components
Header shows 'Latence Réseau' in uppercase with amber color
Current latency displayed in top-right corner with color coding
Current latency shows '--' when offline/null
Latency colors: green (<100ms), yellow (100-300ms), red (>300ms)
Legend at bottom shows color indicators with ranges
Legend shows: green '< 100ms', yellow '100-300ms', red '> 300ms'
Container has stone-900/60 background with stone-700/50 border
Padding matches existing component spacing (p-4)
steps_to_verify: Verify section background color matches stone-900/60
Check border color is stone-700/50
Header text reads 'Latence Réseau'
Header has text-amber-400/80 color and uppercase styling
Test with null latency - verify '--' displays in stone-500 color
Test with 50ms - verify green-400 color and '50 ms' text
Test with 200ms - verify yellow-400 color
Test with 400ms - verify red-400 color
Legend shows three colored dots with correct labels

items.description: Integrate PingGraph into App.tsx below server address section
acceptance_criteria: Replace useServerStatus hook with usePingLatency hook in App
Remove 5-second polling from original hook (now handled by usePingLatency)
PingGraph component renders below server address section
Graph appears before the 'How to Join' instructions
Margin spacing matches existing components (mt-6)
PlayerList and other components continue to receive serverStatus data
Loading and error states are preserved
App maintains medieval theme throughout
steps_to_verify: Check App.tsx imports usePingLatency instead of useServerStatus
Verify PingGraph is rendered in the component tree
Check PingGraph is positioned after server address div
Check PingGraph is before the 'Comment Rejoindre' section
Verify mt-6 class is applied to PingGraph container
Confirm PlayerList still receives players data from serverStatus
Test loading state - verify LoadingView still appears
Test error state - verify error banner still appears

items.description: Ensure 1-second updates don't cause performance issues
acceptance_criteria: No memory leaks from interval or data accumulation
Data array is strictly limited to 300 points maximum
Previous fetch requests are aborted when new ones start
Chart re-renders efficiently without full page refresh
CPU usage remains reasonable during continuous operation
steps_to_verify: Monitor memory usage in DevTools for 5 minutes - verify no growth
Check that data array length never exceeds 300
Verify AbortController aborts previous requests
Use React DevTools Profiler to verify minimal re-renders
Monitor CPU usage - should stay below 10% on modern hardware
